question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can a borrower who fully repays their debt be blocked from withdrawing collateral when a non-withdrawn activated collateral's oracle reverts, under the precondition that the borrower repays all debt (units = debtOf(id, borrower)), then calls withdrawCollateral, trigger the call sequence repayAndWithdrawCollateral -> Midnight.repay (debt = 0) -> withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy -> debt = 0 -> isHealthy returns true WITHOUT calling any oracle (line 948: if (debt > 0) {...}) -> withdrawal succeeds, violating the expectation that oracle revert blocks withdrawal, causing scoped impact: this is actually safe (no oracle called when debt = 0), but fuzz test should confirm no edge case where debt is not fully zeroed? Proof idea: fuzz units = debtOf(id, borrower), assert withdrawCollateral succeeds even with RevertingOracle after full repayment.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can a borrower be forced to pay a referral fee that reduces units repaid below their actual debt, leaving residual debt that causes the subsequent withdrawCollateral to fail the health check, under the precondition that referralFeePct > 0, assets = debtOf(id, borrower), units = assets - referralFeeAssets < debtOf(id, borrower), trigger the call sequence repayAndWithdrawCollateral -> units = assets - assets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD) < debt -> Midnight.repay(units) -> residual debt > 0 -> withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy -> maxDebt < residual_debt -> UnhealthyBorrower revert, violating the invariant that a borrower who passes sufficient assets to cover debt must be able to withdraw collateral, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's withdrawal reverts unexpectedly due to referral fee reducing repaid units? Proof idea: fuzz referralFeePct in [1, WAD-1], assets = debtOf(id, borrower), assert withdrawCollateral reverts when referralFeeAssets > 0.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / maxDebt] Can an unhealthy borrower avoid liquidation when oracle prices for all activated collaterals are exactly at the health threshold, causing maxDebt to round up to exactly debt due to mulDivDown rounding, under the precondition that collateral[i].mulDivDown(price[i], ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv[i], WAD) = debt for all i combined, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> maxDebt = debt -> originalDebt > maxDebt is false -> NotLiquidatable() revert, violating the invariant that a borrower at exactly the health threshold must be liquidatable (or not), causing scoped impact: invalid borrower stays healthy due to rounding making maxDebt == debt? Proof idea: fuzz collateral, price, lltv to find inputs where mulDivDown(mulDivDown(c, p, 1e36), lltv, 1e18) = debt exactly, assert liquidate reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / seizedAssets path] Can a liquidator seize more collateral than the oracle price justifies when liquidatedCollatPrice is very small, under the precondition that oracle[collateralIndex].price() = 1 (minimum), seizedAssets is large, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> liquidatedCollatPrice = 1 -> repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(1, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) = 0 (rounds to 0 for seizedAssets < ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE) -> liquidator seizes collateral for 0 repaid, violating the invariant that liquidation profit must be bounded by maxLif, causing scoped impact: invalid borrower's collateral seized for free due to oracle price near 0? Proof idea: set oracle.price() = 1, seizedAssets = ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE - 1, assert repaidUnits = 0 and liquidation succeeds.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / seizedAssets path] Can a liquidator be forced to repay more than expected when liquidatedCollatPrice is very high, under the precondition that oracle[collateralIndex].price() is near type(uint128).max / collateral, seizedAssets is small, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> liquidatedCollatPrice = very_high -> repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(very_high, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) >> seizedAssets -> liquidator must repay much more than collateral value, violating the invariant that liquidation must be profitable (repaidUnits <= seizedAssets * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * maxLif / WAD), causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to repaidUnits exceeding borrower's debt? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near max, seizedAssets = 1, assert repaidUnits > debt -> _position.debt underflow revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral / Midnight.withdrawCollateral] Can a lender who is also a borrower have their collateral withdrawal blocked after a successful take when the oracle price drops between the take and the withdrawCollateral call within the same bundle transaction, under the precondition that the lender has debt > 0, the take increases their credit, and the oracle price drops (via a callback in the take) to make the position unhealthy, trigger the call sequence buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral -> take (with buyer callback that drops oracle price) -> withdrawCollateral -> isHealthy -> maxDebt < debt -> UnhealthyBorrower revert -> entire tx reverts, violating the invariant that a lender's collateral withdrawal must not be blocked by oracle manipulation in a take callback, causing scoped impact: valid lender's withdrawal reverts unexpectedly due to oracle manipulation in take callback? Proof idea: implement IBuyCallback that calls oracle.setPrice(0), assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget / Midnight.take] Can a borrower's take be blocked when the oracle price for their newly supplied collateral is exactly at the health threshold after the take, under the precondition that the borrower supplies exactly enough collateral to be healthy after the take, but mulDivDown rounding makes maxDebt = debt - 1, trigger the call sequence supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget -> supplyCollateral -> take -> isHealthy -> maxDebt = collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) = debt - 1 -> SellerIsLiquidatable revert -> take reverts -> OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that a borrower who supplies sufficient collateral must be able to borrow, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's borrow via bundle reverts unexpectedly due to rounding at health boundary? Proof idea: fuzz collateral, price, lltv to find inputs where mulDivDown rounds down to debt - 1, assert bundle reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / market.collateralParams] Can isHealthy revert with an array out-of-bounds error when the collateralBitmap has a bit set at index i but market.collateralParams.length <= i, under the precondition that the market struct passed to isHealthy has fewer collateralParams than the bitmap's highest set bit, trigger the call sequence isHealthy -> bitmap loop -> i = msb(bitmap) -> market.collateralParams[i] -> array out of bounds revert, violating the invariant that isHealthy must not revert for valid market/borrower combinations, causing scoped impact: valid borrower's withdrawal/liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to market struct mismatch? Proof idea: unit test where borrower has collateral at index 5 but market.collateralParams.length = 3, assert isHealthy reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / market.collateralParams] Can liquidate revert with an array out-of-bounds error when the collateralBitmap has a bit set at index i but market.collateralParams.length <= i, under the precondition that the market struct passed to liquidate has fewer collateralParams than the bitmap's highest set bit, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> bitmap loop -> i = msb(bitmap) -> market.collateralParams[i] -> array out of bounds revert, violating the invariant that liquidation must not revert for unhealthy positions, causing scoped impact: liquidation of unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly due to market struct mismatch? [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / badDebt / oracle price 0] Can a liquidation with both seizedAssets = 0 and repaidUnits = 0 succeed when oracle returns 0, realizing bad debt without any token transfer, under the precondition that oracle[i].price() = 0 for all activated collaterals, trigger the call sequence liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) -> bitmap loop -> maxDebt = 0 -> badDebt = originalDebt -> _position.debt -= badDebt -> lossFactor updated -> no token transfer, violating the invariant that bad debt realization must accurately reflect collateral value, causing scoped impact: invalid borrower's bad debt realized incorrectly (too much) when oracle returns 0? Proof idea: set oracle.price() = 0, call liquidate(0, 0, ...), assert debtOf = 0 and lossFactor = type(uint128).max.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / LLTV = WAD] Can a liquidation revert unexpectedly when LLTV = WAD (1e18) and the RCF check divides by zero, under the precondition that lltv = WAD, lif = maxLif, and repaidUnits > 0, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> maxRepaid = lltv < WAD ? ... : type(uint256).max -> maxRepaid = type(uint256).max -> repaidUnits <= maxRepaid always true -> no RCF revert, violating the expectation that LLTV = WAD causes division by zero in WAD*WAD - lif*lltv, causing scoped impact: liquidation behavior at LLTV = WAD boundary? Proof idea: unit test with LLTV_8 = WAD, assert liquidate does not revert with division by zero (confirmed by testLiquidatePreMaturityLltvWad).

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / RCF / oracle price] Can the RCF check be bypassed when the oracle price is very low, allowing full liquidation of a position that should only be partially liquidated, under the precondition that oracle[collateralIndex].price() is very small, collateral.mulDivDown(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, lif).zeroFloorSub(maxRepaid) < rcfThreshold, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> RCF check: expression < rcfThreshold -> RCF deactivated -> full liquidation allowed -> repaidUnits = debt -> position fully liquidated, violating the invariant that the RCF must prevent over-liquidation of healthy-enough positions, causing scoped impact: valid borrower over-liquidated due to oracle price making RCF inactive? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price in [1, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE/2], rcfThreshold > 0, assert full liquidation succeeds when it should be partial.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate / RCF / oracle price] Can the RCF check incorrectly block a valid partial liquidation when the oracle price is very high, making the RCF expression very large, under the precondition that oracle[collateralIndex].price() is very large, collateral.mulDivDown(liquidatedCollatPrice, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, lif).zeroFloorSub(maxRepaid) >= rcfThreshold, trigger the call sequence liquidate -> RCF check: expression >= rcfThreshold -> RecoveryCloseFactorConditionsViolated revert, violating the invariant that a valid partial liquidation must not be blocked by the RCF, causing scoped impact: liquidation of unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly due to high oracle price enforcing RCF? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * 1000, repaidUnits = maxRepaid + 1, rcfThreshold = 0, assert liquidate reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.take / isHealthy / liquidationLocked] Can a seller avoid the health check entirely when liquidationLocked(id, seller) is true at the time of the health check, allowing an unhealthy seller to take offers, under the precondition that the seller is already in a nested take (liquidationLocked = true), and the outer take's health check short-circuits to true, trigger the call sequence outer_take -> tExchange(lock=true) -> seller callback -> inner_take -> tExchange(wasLocked=true, keeps lock) -> inner_take health check: liquidationLocked = true -> short-circuit -> inner_take succeeds -> seller debt increases -> outer_take health check: liquidationLocked = false -> isHealthy called -> maxDebt < debt -> SellerIsLiquidatable, violating the invariant that a seller must be healthy after all takes, causing scoped impact: invalid borrower stays healthy during nested take due to liquidation lock short-circuit? Proof idea: implement nested take in seller callback, assert outer take reverts with SellerIsLiquidatable.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IMidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy / oracle price near ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE] Can a borrower with collateral = 1 and oracle.price() = ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE appear healthy when debt = 1, under the precondition that collateral = 1, price = ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, lltv = WAD, trigger the call sequence isHealthy -> maxDebt = 1.mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(WAD, WAD) = 1 -> maxDebt = 1 >= debt = 1 -> healthy, violating no invariant (this is correct), but fuzz test should confirm boundary: collateral = 1, price = ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE - 1, lltv = WAD -> maxDebt = 0 -> unhealthy, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with 1 wei collateral at price ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE - 1 becomes liquidatable? Proof idea: unit test with collateral = 1, price = ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE - 1, lltv = WAD, debt = 1, assert isHealthy returns false.
